title: mulgedium umbrosum
intro: Mulgedium umbrosum, also known as mullein, is a biennial plant species in the figwort family, characterized by its large, velvety leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. Mullein has been used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which make it effective in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of mullein include soothing coughs, reducing inflammation, and promoting relaxation, making it a popular ingredient in teas, tinctures, and topical creams. Mullein can be prepared in various forms, including dried leaves for teas, tinctures made by infusing the leaves in a solvent, and infused oils for topical application.
